summary: This source is a collection description and finding aid for the Andrew Carnegie Collections at Carnegie Mellon University. It provides a brief biographical overview of Carnegie's transition from steel industrialist to philanthropist, highlighting his focus on funding education, libraries, and peace initiatives, as well as detailing the archival papers of his personal secretary, James Bertram.

principles.statement: A first-class technical school is essential to develop the latent talent of future generations.
principles.rationale: Carnegie believed that investing in technical education would unlock the potential of the youth in the Pittsburgh area, leading him to fund the creation of a school of technology.
principles.source_quote: Carnegie stated that a first class technical school would develop the latent talent of future generations in the Pittsburgh area.
